g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I inspect data before K-Means? What plots help?')
g.text: Plot distributions (histograms), detect skewness, find missing values, check outliers with boxplots

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How should I handle missing values before clustering?')
g.text: Impute (median for skewed, mean for normal) or drop rows. K-Means cannot handle NaN.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I encode categorical features for K-Means?')
g.text: One-hot for nominal (e.g. city), ordinal encoding for ordered. Or use K-Prototypes for mixed data.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why should I drop correlated features before K-Means?')
g.text: Drop or combine highly correlated features. Duplicate info distorts Euclidean distance unfairly.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('When should I use log transformation before clustering?')
g.text: Apply log (or log1p if zeros) to right-skewed data. Brings outliers closer to the mean.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Which scaler should I use for K-Means?')
g.text: Data is roughly normal, few outliers

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I find the optimal K for K-Means using elbow method and silhouette?')
g.text: Elbow method (inertia vs K) + Silhouette Score. Pick K at the bend; higher silhouette = better.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What are best practices when running K-Means fit?')
g.text: Run K-Means with n_init=10+ and multiple random seeds (init="k-means++") to avoid local minima.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I evaluate K-Means cluster quality?')
g.text: Silhouette Score, Davies-Bouldin Index, visual PCA/t-SNE plots of cluster separation.

svg.text: Click any step to ask a follow-up question
